commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `.github/workflows/ci.yml` so `check-lint`, `check-additional-extension-bundled`, and `check-additional-extension-package-boundary` extract a GitHub source tarball instead of running the existing git checkout step.

PR surface: Config +128. Total +128 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is a CI automation experiment, not a user-facing bug report. The required proof is successful live execution of the changed workflow lanes.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is appropriate because the remaining blocker is contributor lane proof plus maintainer acceptance of the CI workflow tradeoff, not a deterministic code repair.

target.title: ci: try source archive checkout for code-only jobs
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

CI spends repeated setup time doing shallow Git checkouts for jobs that appear to only need source files.

## Why This Change Was Made

This draft experiments with replacing selected code-only CI checkouts with GitHub source archive download and extraction:

- non-dist Node test shards
- `check-lint`
- `check-additional-boundaries-a`
- `check-additional-extension-bundled`
- `check-additional-extension-package-boundary`

The goal is to measure whether avoiding Git metadata improves wall time for high-frequency CI jobs.

## User Impact

No product behavior changes. This only changes CI checkout mechanics for selected jobs.

## Evidence

Local sanity:

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/ci.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'`

Known draft validation risks to resolve before ready-for-review:

- node tooling shards may still need `.git`
- `check-additional-boundaries-a` may rely on `.git` for repo-root discovery
